subject: log4j security upgrade
commit_message:
log4j security upgrade

Upgrade log4j to fix security risk identified by blackduck scan

Issue-ID: POLICY-2422
Signed-off-by: Joseph Chou <jc2555@att.com>
Change-Id: I465a4f207da787d9c94044fc085c142ae03f6e8a

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-parent-master-verify-java/268/

Patch Set 1:

Please review this change log4j jar to 2.8.2, log4j 1.2.17 was identified as security risk by blackduck scan.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-parent-master-verify-java/268/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-parent-master-verify-java/268
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 1:

Do you mind to amend the commit message title to  "log4j security upgrade" or something that gets an indication of the work (rather than "onap code update)" ?  Thank you!
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2

We are done with security upgrades for Frankfurt - did you even compile ALL the repositories to ensure they don't break with this change?

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2
> 
> We are done with security upgrades for Frankfurt - did you even compile ALL the repositories to ensure they don't break with this change?

Will take a look and make sure all repositories are able to build

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-2
> > 
> > We are done with security upgrades for Frankfurt - did you even compile ALL the repositories to ensure they don't break with this change?
> 
> Will take a look and make sure all repositories are able to build

Ok - this would be good to get in. I specifically didn't upgrade this because I was concerned that across the repos would cause many problems. And I'm not sure the package name group/artifact is correct, but maybe it is. Especially concerned about policy/engine - however we could just for that repo override the version on it just to keep it going.

Please keep me up-to-date on this.

I think these changes may only affect policy/engine.   I don't think other repos use log4j unless transitively in some dependency.

Patch Set 2:

well right now policy/common doesn't even compile with this. Because the artifact details are wrong - according to the vulnerability you have to upgrade to a different package.

Again it would help to compile this with ALL the repos.

Patch Set 2:

> well right now policy/common doesn't even compile with this.
 > Because the artifact details are wrong - according to the
 > vulnerability you have to upgrade to a different package.
 > 
 > Again it would help to compile this with ALL the repos.

yes definitely, all repos must compile as the very minimum.

Patch Set 2:

<!-- https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.apache.logging.log4j/log4j-core -->
<dependency>
    <groupId>org.apache.logging.log4j</groupId>
    <artifactId>log4j-core</artifactId>
    <version>2.13.1</version>
</dependency>


And then policy/common (at the least) needs to be updated to use the right artifact.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

Needs to be evaluated for compatibility.   Documentation mentions about setting some flags to be compatible with 1.x versions

https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/compatibility.html

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

could we leave it as is (i.e., old version) and then remove log4j in G?
